title: teucrium chamaedrys subsp. lydium
intro: Teucrium chamaedrys subsp. lydium, also known as the 'Lydia Germander', is a subspecies of the Germander plant, which is a low-growing, shrubby perennial with small, fragrant leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a sedative, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Teucrium chamaedrys subsp. lydium include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
